title: Charred Comforting Thai Tiger Prawns Pad See Ew
description: A charred, snappy, comforting Thai dish featuring raw tiger prawns, served in a style inspired by traditional Bangkok street food.

ingredients.name: Bangkok street-style sauce
ingredients.notes: see recipe note for DIY version

ingredients.name: Pad see ew noodles
ingredients.notes: rice stick noodles or substitute with regular rice noodles

instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large wok or frying pan over high heat.
Add the tiger prawns and cook for 2 minutes per side, or until charred and just cooked through.
Remove the prawns from the wok and set aside.
In the same wok, add the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil.
Add the chopped shallots and cook for 2 minutes, or until fragrant.
Add the pad see ew noodles and cook for 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add the Bangkok street-style sauce and stir to combine.
Return the cooked prawns to the wok and toss with the noodles and sauce.
Season with ground black pepper and salt to taste.
Transfer the dish to a serving platter and garnish with chopped Thai basil and charred lime wedges.
Recipe note: to make a DIY version of Bangkok street-style sauce, combine 1/4 cup of tamarind paste, 1/4 cup of fish sauce, 2 tablespoons of palm sugar, and 2 tablespoons of water in a saucepan.
Bring to a simmer over medium heat, whisking constantly, and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened.
Let cool and store in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 1 week.
